meta.purpose: Terminology boundaries, traps, and self-checks for Animation Review Master.
meta.load_condition: Load with Core for any generation, review, or refactor task in this domain.

standard_terms.definition: The sequence and timing of visual focus points that guide the viewer through the scene. A broken attention path is the most common animation failure.
The number of distinct motion groups active at any given moment. Density above 3 almost always degrades perceived quality.
A deliberate pause in animation that lets the viewer comprehend information before the next motion beat. Missing holds are a signature of AI-generated animation.
The emotional quality conveyed by the easing curve: power-out = confident, elastic = playful, linear = mechanical/cheap.
Feedback that identifies the root cause of a quality problem (attention, timing, density, easing, performance) rather than describing the symptom ('it looks bad').

banned_terms.why: Vague quality judgments without structural diagnosis are useless for improvement. Translate 'feels off' into: 'three simultaneous motion groups compete for attention at 0.3s.'
banned_terms.replace_with: Replace with specific criteria from the domain ontology.

banned_terms.why: Usually means 'add bounce or scale' without understanding the underlying structural problem. Diagnose what is actually wrong before prescribing an effect.
banned_terms.replace_with: Replace with specific criteria from the domain ontology.

banned_terms.why: The structural causes of AI-looking animation are missing holds, uniform easing, excessive motion groups, and absent attention hierarchy. Critique those, not the origin.
banned_terms.replace_with: Replace with specific criteria from the domain ontology.

misunderstandings.wrong: Animation critique is subjective opinion and cannot be made systematic.
misunderstandings.correct: While the highest level of taste is subjective, 90% of animation quality problems have objective structural causes: attention conflict, density overrun, missing holds, easing mismatch, and performance issues.
misunderstandings.key_distinction: While the highest level of taste is subjective, 90% of animation quality problems have objective structural causes: attention conflict, density overrun, missing holds, easing mismatch, and performance issues.
misunderstandings.why: Holding the wrong interpretation leads to poor judgment: While the highest level of taste is subjective, 90% of animation quality problems have objective structural causes: attention conflict, density overrun, missing holds, easing mismatch, and performance issues.

misunderstandings.wrong: Listing every visible issue produces the best critique.
misunderstandings.correct: Fixing the top 1-2 structural problems often cascades into fixing the rest. A focused critique with evidence is more actionable than a comprehensive list without priorities.
misunderstandings.key_distinction: Fixing the top 1-2 structural problems often cascades into fixing the rest. A focused critique with evidence is more actionable than a comprehensive list without priorities.
misunderstandings.why: Holding the wrong interpretation leads to poor judgment: Fixing the top 1-2 structural problems often cascades into fixing the rest. A focused critique with evidence is more actionable than a comprehensive list without priorities.

self_check: Was the critique framed against the viewer's primary task, not abstract animation principles?
Does every quality claim in the critique trace back to a specific structural cause (attention, timing, density, easing, or performance)?
Are fixes prioritized by severity, starting with the top 1-2 issues?
Did the first recommended fix attempt to remove or reduce motion rather than add more?
